Thy powers, to find out paths to ruin, and let Out the floods of sorrow to o’erwhelm mankind, How feeble is thy sceptre 1 — how uncertain man's- Decisions drawn from thy still remaining light ! Thy beams ere long must sink in night, and Chaos reign again, hut for the power and love Of Him who gave thee; and, by revelation from His own perfected reason, shall he lead thy steps,. For although a man may have many revelations, and have power to do many mighty works, yet if he boasts in his own strength, and sets at naught the counsels of God, and follows after the dictates of his own will and carnal desires, he must fall and incur the vengeance of a just God upon him.
